response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack engineer, but the profile does not match a mid-senior C++ capital-markets role. C++ exposure is minimal and dated — roughly six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) plus a university thesis — far short of the required 5–10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows. There is no SQL Server/Oracle experience (only MySQL/MongoDB/generic SQL), and no fixed-income or capital-markets domain work paired with C++ (the RIDE Capital fintech stint was Node/TypeScript, not C++). Communication skills and engineering discipline are strong, but the core mandatory requirements are unmet.
strengths: Broad, deep software engineering experience with strong TDD/CI/CD and craftsmanship practices
Demonstrated communication ability through workshops, talks, and teaching
Some prior C/C++ exposure at National Instruments and in a C++ audio thesis project
concerns: C++ experience is brief (~6 months) and a decade old, not the required 5–10 years on Windows
No SQL Server/Oracle experience and no fixed-income or capital-markets C++ domain work
Career focus is web/full-stack (TypeScript, Node, DLang), misaligned with the role
